title: dianthus collinus subsp. glabriusculus
intro: Dianthus collinus subsp. glabriusculus, also known as alpine pink or mountain pink, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the carnation family (Caryophyllaceae) that is native to the mountainous regions of Europe. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The plant is believed to have several health benefits, such as reducing stress and anxiety, improving sleep quality, and boosting the immune system. In herbal preparations, the flowers and leaves of Dianthus collinus subsp. glabriusculus can be used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat various health conditions, including digestive issues and skin problems.
